text.title: rag-eval-production-monitoring Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rag-eval-production-monitoring as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using in-memory metrics storage in production across service restarts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: In-memory storage is lost on every restart, deploy, or crash. Production monitoring requires persistent storage (Postgres, InfluxDB, or a dedicated observability platform) to build the historical baselines that anomaly detection needs.

failure-mode.description: Not logging negative user feedback because "users rarely give feedback".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Even sparse feedback is the highest-quality signal. Three negative ratings on the same query type is a stronger signal of systematic failure than a drop in automated faithfulness scores, because it reflects actual user impact rather than LLM evaluator bias.
